title: Detect and Merge Duplicate Tableau Pulse Metrics into Existing Glossary
story: As a data analyst, I want the system to detect when a Tableau Pulse metric already exists in my glossary and offer to merge it, so that I avoid creating duplicate metric entries.
description:
When connecting and adding metrics from Tableau Pulse, the system should check if the metric already exists in the DataTools Pro metrics glossary. Detection uses a case-insensitive exact match against metric names and aliases.

### Behavior
- During fetch and save, each Tableau metric is checked against existing metric names and aliases
- **Suggested metrics**: New metrics that don't match any existing entries
- **Ignored metrics**: Metrics that match an existing name or alias

### UI
- Tiles showing count of total metrics, suggested, and ignored
- Suggested metrics list with option to ignore individual metrics
- Ignored metrics list with option to revert back to suggested
- Merge button to combine a Tableau metric with an existing glossary entry
acceptance_criteria: System checks each Tableau Pulse metric against existing metric names and aliases (case-insensitive)
Metrics matching an existing name or alias are marked as 'ignored'
Non-matching metrics appear in the 'suggested' list
Tiles show counts for total, suggested, and ignored metrics
Users can move metrics between suggested and ignored lists
Merge button allows combining a Tableau metric with an existing glossary entry
ux_changes: New metric ingestion UI with suggested/ignored/merged tiles, sortable lists, and merge workflow matching the existing metric analyst pattern.
middleware_changes: Case-insensitive matching logic against metric names and aliases during Tableau Pulse metric import. Suggested/ignored/merge state management.
api_changes: Extended Tableau Pulse import endpoint to include deduplication logic and merge capability.
implementation_notes: Developer sequenced this after MBT-835 to have access to the Tableau metrics. Matching uses lowercase exact match on both metric name and alias fields. Future enhancement will use a web service for more sophisticated analysis.
comment_summary: Developer completed the implementation with tiles for suggested/ignored metrics, merge functionality, and deduplication. Screenshots provided showing the three-tile interface and merge workflow.
